expectations.evidence: No AGENTS.md file was found anywhere in the outputs directory or in /tmp/eval-monorepo-baseline2. The run created knowledge/project.md and packages/api/knowledge/api.md but did not create AGENTS.md at any level.

expectations.text: Root CLAUDE.md is a symlink to AGENTS.md — check /tmp/eval-monorepo-baseline2/CLAUDE.md
expectations.evidence: /tmp/eval-monorepo-baseline2/CLAUDE.md does not exist. No symlink was created. 'ls -la /tmp/eval-monorepo-baseline2/' shows no CLAUDE.md file or symlink.
No AGENTS.md was found under packages/api/ in either the outputs directory or /tmp/eval-monorepo-baseline2/packages/api/. The api package only has a knowledge/ directory with api.md.
/tmp/eval-monorepo-baseline2/packages/api/CLAUDE.md does not exist. No symlink was created at this path.

expectations.text: Root AGENTS.md links to per-package AGENTS.md files
expectations.evidence: Root AGENTS.md does not exist, so this expectation cannot be satisfied.

expectations.text: packages/api/knowledge/ has at least one doc
expectations.evidence: packages/api/knowledge/api.md exists and contains substantive content: package identity, authentication details (JWT), rate limiting (100 req/min per user, 1000 req/min per IP), key files table, and dependencies.

expectations.text: A project-structure.md exists at either root or package level describing directory layout
expectations.evidence: No file named project-structure.md was found. The knowledge/project.md file does contain a workspace layout section with a directory tree, but the filename does not match the expected 'project-structure.md'.

expectations.text: Root AGENTS.md Task Routing uses conditional form ('如果要…，先读…'), not question form
expectations.evidence: Root AGENTS.md does not exist, so this expectation cannot be evaluated.

expectations.text: Root AGENTS.md covers cross-package concerns; api AGENTS.md focuses on api-specific content
expectations.evidence: Root AGENTS.md does not exist. While the knowledge docs do follow a reasonable separation (knowledge/project.md covers cross-package concerns including shared types and workspace layout, packages/api/knowledge/api.md is api-specific), neither file is named AGENTS.md so the expectation is not met.

claims.claim: The run created knowledge documentation for the monorepo
claims.evidence: knowledge/project.md and packages/api/knowledge/api.md were created with substantive content. project.md covers workspace layout, shared types, technology stack. api.md covers auth, rate limiting, key files.

claims.claim: The executor followed the AGENTS.md / CLAUDE.md convention
claims.evidence: No AGENTS.md or CLAUDE.md files were created at any level. The executor created knowledge/ directories with .md docs, but ignored the AGENTS.md/CLAUDE.md file structure that is central to the task.
api.md line 43 references: 'This package is part of the my-platform monorepo (root knowledge/project.md for cross-package concerns).' and line 44 notes shared type dependency constraints.

suggestions.assertion: A project-structure.md exists at either root or package level describing directory layout
suggestions.reason: The assertion requires a specific filename 'project-structure.md' but the task did not specify this filename — the executor created 'project.md' which contains directory layout info. The assertion should either specify the expected filename precisely in the prompt or check for content (directory tree present in any knowledge doc) rather than a specific filename.

suggestions.assertion: packages/api/knowledge/ has at least one doc
suggestions.reason: This assertion is the only one that passed, but it would also pass for a trivially empty or off-topic file. Consider adding a content-quality check, e.g., 'api/knowledge doc mentions authentication or rate limiting' to ensure the doc actually captures api-specific knowledge.

No assertion checks whether the knowledge files contain accurate content derived from the actual source code. The executor could create placeholder docs that pass all structural checks without actually reading the source. Consider adding: 'packages/api/knowledge doc mentions JWT authentication' or 'mentions rate limiting thresholds'.

The fundamental failure mode here — creating knowledge/ docs instead of AGENTS.md files — went completely undetected by assertions 6 and 7. The eval should include a negative check: 'No files exist in outputs other than AGENTS.md, CLAUDE.md, and knowledge/ docs' or require that AGENTS.md actually imports/references the knowledge docs.

eval_feedback.overall: The run completely missed the AGENTS.md/CLAUDE.md file structure requirement, creating knowledge/ directories instead. 8 of 9 assertions fail. The one passing assertion (api/knowledge has a doc) is the weakest assertion in the set. The eval could be strengthened by checking file content accuracy and adding a check that verifies the AGENTS.md → knowledge/ reference chain works end-to-end.
